channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tatum Bank Achieves CBN Recapitalization Target, Boosting Financial Strength
item.description: Tatum Bank don announce say e don meet the recapitalization milestone wey the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) put for hand of financial institutions inside the country. This one mean say the bank don me...

item.title: KPS Magnet School Pilot Faces Opposition as Few Students Enrol, Teachers Issued Notices
item.description: The pilot project of the Karnataka Public School (KPS) Magnet School in Honganuru, Bengaluru South district, has not progressed as intended due to strong opposition from parents and villagers. The gov...

item.title: Bank of England to Feature Native Wildlife on Next Banknotes
item.description: The Bank of England is making a big shift in the design of its banknotes. Moving away from portraits of historical figures like Winston Churchill, Jane Austen, and William Shakespeare, the next series...

item.title: UST secures fifth consecutive win by defeating UP in UAAP Volleyball
item.description: The University of Santo Tomas (UST) Tigresses have continued their impressive performance in the UAAP volleyball season by securing their fifth consecutive victory, this time against the University of...

item.title: Sam Docherty’s candid voice memo exposes pressures on AFL coaches and clubs
item.description: Early this AFL season, Sam Docherty’s voice memo went viral for its frank, colourful criticism of his former club, Carlton. Delivered in an unusually candid tone, Docherty highlighted frustrations tha...

item.title: Comelec explains delays in resolving Marcoleta’s campaign finance case
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) disclosed the complexities causing the delay in the resolution of Senator Rodante Marcoleta’s case regarding alleged discrepancies in his Statement of Contributio...

item.title: Arteta confirms Saliba available, Odegaard out for Leverkusen clash
item.description: Arsenal go face Bayer Leverkusen today for the first leg of their Champions League last-16 match. Manager Mikel Arteta don confirm say centre-back William Saliba don fully recover from him ankle injur...

item.title: Rivers State Ministry Launches Programme to Educate Teens on Preventing Teenage Pregnancies
item.description: The Rivers State Ministry of Health don waka go schools to educate adolescents about the wahala of teenage pregnancies. Mrs. Tammy Briggs, Programme Manager for Adolescent Health and Development, yarn...

item.title: UK Banknotes to Feature Native Wildlife Instead of Historical Figures
item.description: The Bank of England has announced that the next generation of British banknotes will replace traditional portraits of historical figures with images of native wildlife. Figures such as Winston Churchi...

item.title: How a Small Gesture Became a Lifelong Lesson from My Mother
item.description: When the author was around ten years old, her mother mentioned the curious skill of raising a single eyebrow, describing it as an actor’s trick for expressing subtle inner thoughts. Intrigued, the aut...

item.title: Clayton Fuller and Shawn Harris Advance to Runoff in Georgia's 14th District Special Election
item.description: In a crowded special election held on March 10, 2026, for Georgia's 14th Congressional District, Republican Clayton Fuller and Democrat Shawn Harris emerged as the top two vote-getters, advancing to a...

item.title: Loyalty rewarded as Tinubu elevates Masari, Saraki calls for PDP unity amid crises
item.description: This political column highlights several key developments in Nigeria's ruling and opposition circles. Former Senate President Bukola Saraki urged PDP leaders to rise above bruised egos and personal in...

item.title: London authorities ban Al Quds march over public safety concerns
item.description: London authorities have prohibited a pro-Iranian march planned for this weekend, citing significant risks of public disorder. The Home Secretary, Shabana Mahmood, approved the Metropolitan Police's re...

item.title: US Ambassador Flags South Africa’s Lack of Response on Bilateral Concerns
item.description: The new US ambassador to South Africa, Leo Brent Bozell III, has warned that Washington is running out of patience with Pretoria due to the South African government’s failure to respond to five key re...

item.title: Tanzania's Investment Market Remains Stable Despite Global Uncertainty
item.description: Even with the world facing tension between big powers like the US and Iran, Tanzania's capital markets are standing strong. Domestic investors are joining in large numbers, with people moving from sim...

item.title: ICC clarifies travel delays for West Indies and South Africa squads after T20 World Cup
item.description: The International Cricket Council (ICC) has officially denied any accusations of bias regarding travel arrangements for the West Indies and South Africa cricket teams, who were stuck in India for over...

item.title: Some Countries Hesitate to Accept Tinubu’s Ambassadorial Nominees Over Remaining Term
item.description: Dem talk say India and some other countries never gree receive certain ambassadors wey President Bola Tinubu recently post. The main reason be say these countries get policy wey no dey allow them acce...

item.title: Government announces support measures for Indian shippers affected by halted West Asia maritime traffic
item.description: The Government of India has rolled out relief measures for Indian shippers whose cargo destined for West Asian ports has been stranded due to the ongoing US-Israel war against Iran. Maritime traffic v...

item.title: Vice Ganda Shares a Funny Moment Between Vhong Navarro and Lassy on It’s Showtime
item.description: On the March 11 episode of 'It’s Showtime,' Vice Ganda created a humorous and lighthearted moment by bringing Vhong Navarro and Lassy close together on stage. The segment, filled with laughter and pla...

item.title: Zanzibar President Mwinyi Urges Leaders to Embrace Justice in Governance
item.description: Zanzibar President Hussein Ali Mwinyi has called upon leaders across Tanzania to govern with justice and fairness as the foundation of good leadership. Speaking at a public event, he emphasized that t...
